title: Rule- and Regime-Gated Experts
summary: Official F1 rules differ materially by regime: dry races require at least two slick compounds, wet races waive that requirement, and the Monaco Grand Prix had a mandatory two-stop rule in 2025.
rationale: Official F1 rules differ materially by regime: dry races require at least two slick compounds, wet races waive that requirement, and the Monaco Grand Prix had a mandatory two-stop rule in 2025. A single global model likely averages incompatible policies, especially with wet laps and pre-season testing mixed into training.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for normal dry slick races, wet/intermediate-wet races, Monaco-2025, and optionally pre-season testing; blend them with a small gate using Race, Year, Compound family, and wet/dry indicators. Compare against a single-model baseline under race-group CV.
expected_effect: Should reduce policy averaging and improve generalization on odd regimes; most likely a modest CV lift with better fold stability.
risk: Small special-regime slices can overfit, especially Monaco-2025 and pre-season testing.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
